text.paragraph: Temporarily relieves these symptoms due to hay fever or other upper respiratory allergies:
Do not use if you have ever had an allergic reaction to this product or any of its ingredients or an antihistamine containing hydroxyzine
Ask a doctor before useif you have liver or kidney disease.
Your doctor should determine if you need a different dose.
Ask a doctor or pharmacist before use if you are taking tranquilizers or sedatives

list.item: •alcohol, sedatives, and tranquilizers may increase drowsiness
•be careful when driving a motor vehicle or operating machinery

text.paragraph: Stop use and ask a doctor if an allergic reaction to this product occurs. Seek medical help right away.

list.item: •if pregnant: ask a health professional before use.

code.displayName: OTC - KEEP OUT OF REACH OF CHILDREN SECTION

section.title: Keep out of reach of children.

text.paragraph: In case of overdose, get medical help or contact a Poison Control Center right away (1-800-222-1222)

td.paragraph: adults and children 6 years and over
1 tablet (5 mg) or 2 tablets (10) once daily depending upon severity of symptoms; do not take more than 2 tablets (10) in 24 hours.
1 tablet (5 mg) once a day; do not take more than 1 tablet (5 mg) in 24 hours.
consumers with liver or kidney disease
adults and children 6 years and over
one 10 mg tablet once daily; do not take more than one 10 mg tablet in 24 hours.
A 5 mg product may be appropriate for less severe symptoms.
consumers with liver or kidney disease

item.content: do not use of imprinted foil inner seal on bottle is broken or missing.

list.item: •FDA approved organic impurities test procedure differs from USP

text.paragraph: colloidal silicon dioxide, croscarmellose sodium, hypromellose, lactose monohydrate, magnesium stearate, microcrystalline cellulose, polyethylene glycol, titanium dioxide
call toll-free weekdays 9 AM to 5 PM EST at 1 (888) 721-7115
